description: On-demand integration health check. Ask 'check my integrations' any time and Runneth runs one cheap read call per connected integration to confirm auth works, writes one durable status file (per-integration status, last-checked date, action needed), and reports in chat. The markdown file is the state — each run compares against the previous one and notes changes. A sentinel-guarded user.md instruction, installed after the first successful check, makes Runneth surface any integration error it hits mid-task instead of working around it silently. No scheduled routine, no Slack posting, no JSON state.

installs.purpose: The single on-demand skill — discovers connected integrations, verifies each with one cheap read call, writes /agent/brain/integration-health/health-status.md, reports in chat

runtime_installs.trigger: After the first successful health check, idempotent across subsequent runs
runtime_installs.purpose: Standing instruction: if an integration auth/connection error gets in the way during any task, tell the user in that reply and suggest running the health check — never work around it silently. Points Runneth at the status file for already-recorded issues.

post_install.name: Introduce the new capability
post_install.description: Read post-install-intro.md from this use case folder. After all prior install and post-install steps complete, surface the file's sections to the user in chat as the closing message of the install turn: '## What just opened up' as prose, '## Try this now' as a numbered prompt list, '## Compounds with' only when present and at least one named use case is installed in the sandbox. Resolve any {{TOKEN}} placeholders using the same customize map applied during install.

changelog.notes: Rebuilt as a single on-demand health check. Collapsed three skills (integration-health-check, setup-health-alerts, integration-onboarding patch) into one: ask 'check my integrations' and Runneth verifies each connected integration with one cheap read call, writes /agent/brain/integration-health/health-status.md, and reports in chat. Cut the auto-invoked setup skill, the 30-minute recurring routine and its self-healing logic, automatic Slack alerts to an admin channel, secret scanning of runtime tokens, routine health monitoring, the conversation intent scan, and all four JSON config/state seed files — the markdown status file is now the only state, with changes noted inline run over run. Added a sentinel-guarded user.md instruction (runneth-apps:health-alerts:surface-integration-issues v2), installed after the first successful check, so Runneth mentions any integration error it hits mid-task and suggests the health check instead of failing silently. Users who want a recurring check can ask Runneth to schedule one themselves; the skill never creates it.
Added post-install intro. After install completes, Runneth surfaces a short 'what just opened up + try this now' message sourced from post-install-intro.md.

Setup skill: full plain-language rewrite, routines-first framing, simplified admin tagging (tell not ask), setup only flags actionable issues not integration lists. Health check skill: Part D routine intent scan (daily, conservative self-resolution with Slack thread reply + web conversation link). All alert messages rewritten to plain language — no jargon, cause in plain language when known. Added use-case.json and marketing.md.

Setup skill: Step 0b backfills already-connected integrations on install. Question 1b resolves admin tagging from workspace-map.json, stored as admin_slack_ids array with channel membership check. Health check skill: Step B1b adds known-routines-registry for silent drop detection.

Renamed from integration-health-alerts to health-alerts. Added routine health monitoring: overdue detection, execution record checks, output freshness validation, dependency chain tracking, unverifiable status with inline fix from Slack alerts. Retroactive skill audit at setup.
